meaning: adj.未卷绕的,松散的 vbl.unwind的过去式和过去分词
examples: The bandage gradually unwound and fell off.
One of them unwound a length of rope from around his waist.
The Princess unwound her long braid, pulled out a single hair and handed it to the littlest knight.
Another striking difference in this case is how quickly it seems to have unwound.
So when those global economic imbalances are eventually unwound, it will hurt that much more.
